stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 3 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 47.8 % (within-trial site SD = 5.0). trial site B has 20 observations with mean 54.7 % (within-trial site SD = 7.9). trial site C has 10 observations with mean 41.4 % (within-trial site SD = 5.4). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.6 and 9.0 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site A: 47.9 % (shrunk from raw 47.8). Range [47.8, 48.0] as between-trial site SD varies from 3.6 to 9.0. Pooled mean: 49.4.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 49.4 % (weighted average of 3 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=6.0: B = 0.956.
Interval [47.8, 48.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (47.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (49.4) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
